description_long: This object is a large, cylindrical stone container with thick walls and a wide, open top. Its exterior surface is composed of coarse, exposed aggregate concrete, giving it a rugged, pebbled texture with visible small stones and granules embedded throughout. The rim is rounded and slightly beveled, providing a smooth transition to the interior, which appears hollow and deep. The coloration is predominantly a mix of dark gray and brown tones, with occasional lighter specks from the aggregate. The overall form is simple and robust, suggesting durability and resistance to outdoor elements. This type of object is commonly used as a planter, waste receptacle, or barrier in urban public spaces.
description: A large, cylindrical stone container with a coarse, pebbled concrete texture and a wide, open top. Its robust build and dark gray coloration suggest use as a planter or urban barrier.
description_view_0: The front view shows the wide, open top and the rounded rim, highlighting the coarse aggregate texture and the depth of the interior.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the cylindrical shape and the consistent, rough stone surface, with the rim and opening still clearly visible.
description_view_2: The side view displays the uniformity of the stone texture and the thickness of the walls, with less emphasis on the opening.
description_view_3: This angle shows the curvature of the cylinder and the even distribution of aggregate material, with the open top partially visible.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or public park as a planter or barrier
attribute_installation_method: placed or set on a flat, stable surface, often freestanding
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears uniformly rough with no moss, chips, or discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, typically freestanding as a barrier or planter
